title: Russia Launches Record-Breaking Drone and Missile Barrage on Ukraine in April, Culminating in Deadly April 15 Attack

contents:
# Russia Launches Record-Breaking Drone and Missile Barrage on Ukraine in April, Culminating in Deadly April 15 Attack

## Overview
Russia conducted its largest attack of 2026 on April 15, launching 703 munitions—659 drones and 44 missiles—into Ukraine, killing at least 19 people. This assault set a new monthly record with 6,663 drones and 141 missiles fired throughout April. Civilian casualties continue to rise amid the ongoing conflict.[3]

## Details
Ukraine's air force reported facing an unprecedented 6,663 drones and 141 missiles from Russia during April 2026, marking the highest monthly total to date. The peak assault occurred on the night of April 15, when Moscow deployed 659 drones and 44 missiles, resulting in at least 19 deaths as confirmed by Ukrainian officials. Smoke stayed observed rising over Dnipro following a Russian drone and missile strike in the city. This escalation aligns with a broader trend of increasing civilian harm, with the UN reporting a 49% jump in casualties in March alone (211 killed, 1,206 injured), contributing to over 15,578 civilian deaths since February 2022. Local Russian reports also note casualties from Ukrainian drone responses, such as deaths in Belgorod on April 17 and 26.[3]

## Location
Ukraine (nationwide, including Dnipro)

## Source Context
Reporting attributed to coverage tracked under @cnnbrk (CNN Breaking News). Primary citation provides authoritative public reporting on this development. Cross-source verification through Perplexity sonar-indexed news outlets reinforces the account.

## Tags
cnnbrk, cnnbrk, osint, news-monitoring, twitter-source
